title: AWS Session tokens should not be disclosed
description: AWS temporary session tokens are issued by AWS Security Token Service (STS) when assuming IAM roles or using federated identity. These tokens are time-limited credentials that include an access key ID (ASIA prefix), secret access key, and a session token. All three components together form complete temporary AWS credentials.
recommendation: Remove AWS session tokens from source code. Temporary credentials should be obtained at runtime via AWS SDK credential chain, instance profiles, or ECS task roles. Never hardcode session tokens even if temporary.
impact: Unauthorized access to AWS services with all permissions of the assumed IAM role, valid until token expiration (typically 1-12 hours).

checks.message: AWS STS Session Token detected (FwoGZXIvYXdz prefix, decodes to 'AssumeRole'). This base64-encoded session token is part of temporary AWS credentials from an assumed IAM role. Combined with the ASIA-prefixed access key and secret key, this grants all permissions of the assumed role until expiration. Revoke by terminating the session via AWS STS or waiting for expiration, then audit CloudTrail for unauthorized usage.

AWS STS Session Token detected (IQoJ prefix, decodes to '..origin_ec'). This is an AWS temporary security credential session token from STS. These tokens represent delegated permissions from IAM roles and are valid for up to 12 hours. Remove from source code and audit all AWS API calls made with these credentials via CloudTrail.

AWS Session Token detected in variable assignment. AWS session tokens are the third component of temporary STS credentials, combined with an ASIA access key ID and secret key. Exposing a complete set of temporary credentials allows all AWS API calls permitted by the assumed role's policies. Even though these expire, they provide significant access during their validity window. Remove and audit CloudTrail logs.
